items.q: Which item is removed first from a stack?
items.why: Students confuse stacks (LIFO - like a stack of plates) with queues (FIFO - like a checkout line). The 'stack of plates' analogy makes LIFO concrete.

items.q: What does in-order traversal of a binary search tree produce?
items.misconception: Nodes in the order they were inserted.
items.why: Students forget the BST property: left subtree less than node less than right subtree. In-order (left, node, right) therefore visits values in sorted order - regardless of insertion order.

items.correct: The ability of different classes to respond to the same method call in their own way.
items.misconception: Having multiple classes with the same name.
items.random_wrong: Variables that change type at runtime.
items.why: Students confuse polymorphism with naming. Polymorphism means 'many forms' - a single interface (method signature) behaving differently depending on the object's actual class.

items.q: Which describes a network in a single building?
items.why: Correct answer: LAN. “WAN” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many layers does the OSI model have?
items.why: The real figure is 7 layers. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
